channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Palestinian officials say 51 killed in Israeli strikes on southern Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel launched ground operations and carried out airstrikes in a hard-hit city in southern Gaza overnight, killing at least 51 people, including women and children, Palestinian medical officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cadaver dogs wade through muck of communities 'wiped off the map' by Helene
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris in the mountains of western North Carolina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai police seek negligence charges for a school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai police on Wednesday said they were investigating whether the school bus fire that killed 23 young students and teachers in suburban Bangkok was caused by negligence after filing initial charges against the driver.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Police in the Danish capital said two predawn explosions took place Wednesday in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting the nearby Jewish school to close for the day.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Women's role in Catholic Church tops agenda as Pope Francis opens second phase of big reform project
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis opened the second phase of his big Catholic reform project Wednesday, with widespread calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility in the church topping the agenda but ordained ministry still ruled out. Francis presided over an opening Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dad treks miles through Hurricane Helene aftermath to walk daughter down aisle on her wedding day
item.description: A father's love knew no boundaries over the weekend as he walked nearly 30 miles through flood debris in South Carolina to Tennessee to walk his daughter down the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nantucket's migrant crisis handling called out after quiet island rocked by wave of violent attacks
item.description: Toby Brown, head of Nantucket's GOP, wants more vetting on the island after illegal migrants accused of sex crimes and assault there were seized by ICE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New details released about inmate's escape in Northern California
item.description: Caleb Duerr, a 39-year-old inmate who escaped from the Plumas County Jail on September 27, was able to manipulate two fenced areas and flee to an O'Reilly's auto parts store.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: 11 workers at a Tennessee factory were swept away in Hurricane Helene flooding. Only 5 were rescued
item.description: As the rain from Hurricane Helene came down harder and harder, workers inside a plastics factory in rural Tennessee kept working. It wasn’t until water flooded into the parking lot and the power went out that the plant shut down and sent workers home. Several never made it.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Jennifer Boehme grew up scouting beaches around her home in St. Petersburg, Florida, for whatever she could find. Rocks, sand dollars, coquina mollusks — anything the ocean gave up. Now, 40 years later, Boehme wants to launch another treasure hunt.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Helene death toll now at least 166 as Biden plans to visit ravaged Carolinas
item.description: President Joe Biden will survey the devastation in North and South Carolina as rescuers continue their search for anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene caused catastrophic damage across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Erik Verduzco, Jeff Amy And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Clues that could have helped solve the Texas Killing Fields murders in an all-new episode of ‘The Evidence Room’
item.description: A new episode of the KPRC 2 Investigates true crime docuseries 'The Evidence Room' premieres Wednesday at 6:30 p.m. exclusively on the KPRC 2+ livestream.
item.dc:creator: Robert Arnold, Tiffani Lupenski, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris and Biden are fanning out across the Southeast as devastation from Helene grows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the last four years, President Joe Biden has jetted off to survey damage and console victims after tornadoes, wildfires and tropical storms. It's not a role that Kamala Harris has played as vice president.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s death is expected to plead guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged as part of the investigation of the overdose death of Matthew Perry is expected to plead guilty in a federal court in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dalton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump is returning this weekend to the town where an assassin's bullet grazed his ear in July.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: A program that helps refugees resettle in the United States has expanded to include LGBTQ+ people who have been threatened and harassed in their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey And Terry Chea, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams is due back in court in his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to return to court Wednesday in a case where he is accused of taking bribes and illegal campaign contributions. The Democrat is set to make a 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: JD Vance: Donald Trump improved Obamacare
item.description: Sen. JD Vance defended Donald Trump's health care record, claiming Trump had improved the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, KRON4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: The US could see shortages and higher retail prices if a dockworkers strike drags on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977. Workers began walking picket lines early Tuesday, picketing near ports all along the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education a potential flashpoint in Vance-Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Education hardly came up in last month’s Trump-Harris presidential debate, but it could be a central focus at Tuesday’s undercard event.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: GREAT FALLS, Mont. (AP) — An 81-year-old Montana man was sentenced Monday to six months in federal prison for illegally using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunting in Texas and Minnesota. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida enacts tough law to get homeless off the streets, leaving cities and counties scrambling
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — As the sun rose Tuesday, Robert Robinson pulled himself from the sidewalk outside Fort Lauderdale's bus depot. It's where he'd slept — and become a violator of Florida's newest law.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets’ for the Halloween season
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween. The Boo Buckets, a customer-favorite which doubles as a trick-or-treating bucket, comes with Happy Meals. The company will introduce a blue bucket this year, replacing last year’s purple one.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Mississippi justices reject latest appeal from man on death row since 1976
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The Mississippi Supreme Court on Tuesday unanimously denied the latest appeal from a man who has been on the state's death row longer than any other inmate.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene's death toll passes 150 as crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris on Tuesday looking in the mountains of western North Carolina for victims of Hurricane Helene, days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel attacked by Iran: American Jewish leaders say Islamist regime will pay for missile strike
item.description: Jewish American leaders on Tuesday afternoon reacted to Iran's unprecedented missile attacks against Israel and threats in that region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KTLA 5

item.title: Publix donates $1 million to support disaster relief following Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida-based grocery chain, Publix, announced it's making a major contribution to help relief efforts in areas affected by the catastrophic damage left behind by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC
